This type of '''Mon Calamari Star Cruiser''' was a vessel used by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="MSWROTJ">[[Return of the Jedi 3|''Return of the Jedi'' 3]]</ref><ref name="EE">''[[Star Wars: Empire's End]]''</ref>

==Characteristics==
It was a type of stocky vessel with an almost cone-like prow. These vessels were almost twice as long as [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigates]], but were much more massive than the skeletal [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]].<ref name="MSWROTJ" />

The model was equipped with landing gear and could dock inside enclosed [[Hangar/Legends|hangars]]. Although about the size of most non-[[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] frigates, these vessels were referred to as "[[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]]."<ref name="MSWROTJ" /><ref name="EE" />

==History==
[[File:MCC_DEII_missile.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A [[Galaxy Gun]] missile embedded into the side of a [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]].]]

Ships of this design participated in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], alongside other distinct classes such as the larger [[MC80 Liberty Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|''Liberty'' type]] and [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|''Home One'' type MC80 Star Cruisers]].<ref name="MSWROTJ" />

Shortly after the battle, some of these vessels were at the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] of [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]], undergoing repairs and maintenance work.<ref name="MSW98">[[Star Wars (1977) 98|''Star Wars'' (1977) 98]]</ref>

In [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], the Mon Calamari still utilized this ship type, as one was docked at the space city in the [[Auril system/Legends|Nespis system]] when it was hit in the side by a missile dud shot from the [[Galaxy Gun]].<ref name="EE" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:MCQ-mccruiser.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[Ralph McQuarrie]] concept art of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].]]

This ship class was first drawn in concept art by [[Ralph McQuarrie]] during the pre-production of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']]. A black and white version of the picture was used to depict Calamari cruisers in [[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi|''Galaxy Guide 5'']] and the design was also seen in the [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (1983)|comic adaption]] of the film.

Later on, it would appear again in [[Star Wars (1977) 98|''Star Wars'' (1977) 98]] and in ''[[Star Wars: Empire's End]]''.
